newsSince: Modestly positive but not transformative: through mid-June, NVIDIA confirmed H200 purchase orders from Chinese customers and a manufacturing restart for that market after US/China clearances, though deliveries remain in regulatory limbo. No new earnings in the window (Q1 FY2027 was late May). Megacap crowding and ETF-ownership remain the binding alpha cap on most pages. -2.9% since June 9.

verdicts.rankRationale: No new node-bottleneck evidence since June 9. Disclosed TSMC/Samsung foundry + SK hynix/Micron memory dependency intact, but megacap crowding and ETF-ownership cap still define this page's alpha discount. Korea commitments already priced. Hold.
verdicts.bull: AI cash-flow king; Vera Rubin ramp 2H26 sustains accelerator demand and disclosed-dependency relevance.
verdicts.neutral: Demand intact but fully recognized; node-criticality story unchanged since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Megacap crowding and extreme ETF ownership cap any alpha on this under-owned-bottleneck lens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Vera Rubin ramp / hyperscaler capex durability

verdicts.rankRationale: Nothing CPO-specific since June 9. NVDA owns demand/system architecture but discovery is extreme and there is no co-packaged-optics catalyst; deep rank on this crowding-sensitive list remains correct.
verdicts.bull: Sets system architecture; CPO adoption strategically routes through NVDA platform.
verdicts.neutral: CPO exposure strategic not pure-play; no new optics datapoint.
verdicts.bear: Maximally discovered megacap; zero residual CPO alpha at this market cap.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Extreme crowding; CPO is peripheral to thesis

verdicts.rankRationale: Clearest listed proxy for the high-end-accelerator silicon bucket; Korea GW-scale sovereign commitments already supported the score on June 9 and no fresh consumption datapoint has landed. Rank 2 already prices proxy status. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Single best proxy for AI-cluster silicon/HBM/networking consumption growth into 2027.
verdicts.neutral: Proxy role intact; no incremental basket-consumption news.
verdicts.bear: Rank 2 fully prices the proxy; market-cap/YTD discipline caps further upside.

verdicts.rankRationale: No new humanoid/physical-AI news since June 9 that changes the hidden-gem calculus this page rewards. NVDA is the platform king, the opposite of an under-covered robotics name, so it stays low at rank 42.
verdicts.bull: Isaac/Omniverse physical-AI stack underpins the robotics ecosystem.
verdicts.neutral: Platform exposure, but not a robotics pure-play.
verdicts.bear: Far too visible to be a robotics hidden gem.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Robotics remains a small revenue sliver

verdicts.rankRationale: Master under-owned-bottleneck list: NVDA scores low because consensus and cap concentration are extreme. No new fundamental evidence since June 9; Korea GW news already modest. Scarce-bottleneck alpha remains capped. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Compute-silicon platform leader with disclosed foundry/memory choke-point exposure.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck exposure real but fully recognized; YTD reset already applied.
verdicts.bear: Most-owned AI name; offers no under-owned bottleneck alpha.
